Container.Name: FanBridge
Container.Overview:
FanBridge reads Unraid disk temperatures, calculates fan demand, and can control one or more external FanBridge Link PWM controllers over USB serial when automatic output is enabled.
Run the container unprivileged. For each controller, select its stable host path under /dev/serial/by-id and map it to a distinct container device (/dev/ttyACM0, /dev/ttyACM1, and so on). Do not expose all of /dev.
Firmware flashing is hard-disabled; update controller firmware manually from a trusted host. On first run, use the setup token written to /config/setup.token and shown once in the container log to create the administrator account.
Container.Description: Unraid disk-temperature monitoring and external PWM fan-controller bridge.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Host port for the FanBridge web interface.

Config.Name: AppData
Config.Description: Persistent configuration, users, session secret, setup token, and history.

Config.Name: Unraid emhttp directory
Config.Description: Read-only directory containing the live disks.ini file. Map the directory, not an overlapping single-file bind.

Config.Name: FanBridge controller 1
Config.Description: Choose this controller's stable host entry under /dev/serial/by-id. It is exposed inside the container as /dev/ttyACM0.

Config.Name: FanBridge controller 2
Config.Description: Optional second controller. Choose a different /dev/serial/by-id entry and configure /dev/ttyACM1 in FanBridge. Add more Device entries with distinct targets for additional boards.

Config.Name: First-run setup token
Config.Description: Optional. Leave blank to generate /config/setup.token and print it once at startup. If preset, use a long random value and treat the saved template as sensitive.

Config.Name: Stale temperature threshold (seconds)
Config.Description: Treat disks.ini as stale after this interval. The recommended 600 seconds allows one missed update when Unraid poll_attributes is 300 seconds.

Config.Name: Secure cookies
Config.Description: Set to 1 only when the Web UI is served through HTTPS; browsers will then send the session cookie over HTTPS only.
